hero.title: Software Engineer
hero.tagline: Software engineer passionate about building reliable, scalable and impactful applications.

about.title: About Me
about.description: Passionate about computing and development — it's both my job and one of my hobbies. I enjoy turning complex problems into elegant solutions, whether that's debugging a segfault at line 459 of a personal project or architecting a production-grade internal tool. Outside of code I fly drones, play piano, build things with a 3D printer and lose myself in video games.

bullets: Development of internal tools and applications (project management, inventory management, production forecasting).
Full-stack development across multiple frameworks and languages: React, Angular, Python, Java.
Cloud infrastructure and deployments on AWS and Azure.
Software development for satellite video transmission (Java, C++).
Software development and scripting (C++, Python, Node.js, MongoDB).
Development of tools for a VR chair (C, Python, C++).
Creation of VR experiences (Unreal Engine – C++, Unity3D – C#).
On-site installation and presentation at trade shows and corporate events (Matignon, Galeries Lafayette, Johnson & Johnson…).

items.description: Peer-to-peer project-based computer science school. No teachers, no courses — learning through practice and collaboration.

Competitive hackathon where small robots armed with balloons fight for territory in a miniature maze. Participated in 2024 and 2025 with two friends — fully designed and programmed the robots under tight computing resource constraints.
This interactive CV — a fully responsive personal website built with React and Material UI, featuring smooth-scroll navigation, EN/FR language switching and a one-click CV download.

contact.title: Get In Touch
contact.subtitle: Have a project in mind or just want to say hello? My inbox is always open.

foodMatchPrivacy.title: FoodMatch
foodMatchPrivacy.subtitle: Privacy Policy
foodMatchPrivacy.overview_p: FoodMatch is a personal meal-planning utility. It helps you track food restrictions, allergies, and dietary preferences for people in your life, and scan food product barcodes to check ingredient compatibility.
foodMatchPrivacy.overview_highlight: FoodMatch does not collect, transmit, or share any personal data. All information you enter stays exclusively on your device.
foodMatchPrivacy.storage_p: All data created within FoodMatch — including people's names and their food restrictions — is stored locally on your device using the device's own secure storage (AsyncStorage). This data is:
foodMatchPrivacy.storage_li3: Permanently deleted when you uninstall the app.
foodMatchPrivacy.camera_p: FoodMatch requests access to your camera solely to scan food product barcodes. Camera frames are processed entirely on-device and are never stored or transmitted.
foodMatchPrivacy.network_p: When you scan a barcode, FoodMatch queries the Open Food Facts public API to retrieve ingredient and allergen information for that product.
foodMatchPrivacy.network_li1: Only the barcode number is sent to Open Food Facts.
foodMatchPrivacy.network_li2: No personal information, names, or restriction profiles are ever transmitted.
foodMatchPrivacy.network_li3: Barcode lookups are subject to the Open Food Facts Privacy Policy.
foodMatchPrivacy.network_cache: Product data retrieved from Open Food Facts is cached locally on your device to reduce repeat network requests.
foodMatchPrivacy.analytics_p: FoodMatch contains no analytics, no advertising, and no tracking of any kind. No SDKs for these purposes are included in the app.
foodMatchPrivacy.children_p: FoodMatch does not collect any data from any users, including children under the age of 13. The app is safe for use by all age groups.
foodMatchPrivacy.changes_p: If this privacy policy is updated, the new version will be published at this URL and the "Last updated" date above will be revised. Material changes will also be noted in the app store release notes.
foodMatchPrivacy.contact_p: If you have any questions about this privacy policy, you can reach the developer at:

stargate.subtitle: Stargate Command — Level 27 & 28 interactive facility map

l27-briefing-room.name: Briefing Room
l27-briefing-room.shortDesc: Where SG teams receive mission briefings from General Hammond.
l27-briefing-room.longDesc: The Briefing Room sits directly above the Control Room and is connected by a staircase to General Hammond's office. It features a large oval table where all active SG teams gather before and after missions. The room's large windows overlook the Stargate below, giving commanders a direct line of sight to the gate during activations. Critical decisions — including whether to engage alien threats, negotiate with new civilizations, or mount rescue operations — are made here.

l27-generals-office.name: General's Office
l27-generals-office.shortDesc: Office of the SGC commanding officer, accessible from the Briefing Room.
l27-generals-office.longDesc: The commanding officer's office is adjacent to the Briefing Room on Level 27. Occupied for most of the series by Major General George Hammond, the room serves as the administrative and command hub of the SGC. It features the general's desk, secure communication lines to the Pentagon and the President, and a personal window overlooking the Briefing Room. Later in the series it becomes the office of General Landry.

l27-elevator.name: Elevator
l27-elevator.shortDesc: Internal elevator connecting Level 27 directly to Level 28 below.
l27-elevator.longDesc: This elevator provides a single-floor connection between Level 27 — the administrative level housing the Briefing Room and General's Office — and Level 28 below, where the Stargate and Control Room are located. It is the fastest route between command and gate operations, and is used constantly by SG team leaders, the base commander, and senior staff during active missions.

l28-gateroom.name: Gate Room
l28-gateroom.shortDesc: The heart of the SGC — houses the Stargate and its ramp.
l28-gateroom.longDesc: The Gateroom is the most iconic location in the SGC. It houses the recovered Ancient Stargate — a 6.7-metre ring of naquadah capable of forming stable wormholes to other planets across the galaxy. The room is dominated by the gate itself, mounted on a raised platform, with a metal ramp leading up to the event horizon. The ceiling retracts to allow the gate to be raised via a crane into the missile silo shaft above. Blast doors protect the control room and adjacent areas when the gate activates. The room is kept under constant armed guard.

l28-control-room.name: Control Room
l28-control-room.shortDesc: Glass-windowed operations room overlooking the Stargate.
l28-control-room.longDesc: Positioned one level above the Gateroom with large reinforced windows, the Control Room is where Sgt. Walter Harriman (and others) operates the dialing computer and monitors all gate activity. The room contains the DHD (Dial-Home Device) computer interface, incoming/outgoing wormhole confirmation systems, and sensor readouts. General Hammond and later General Landry frequently observe gate activations from this room. It is connected directly to the Briefing Room above via the internal staircase.

l28-blast-door.name: Blast Door
l28-blast-door.shortDesc: Massive reinforced doors protecting the interior from gate energy.
l28-blast-door.longDesc: The blast doors are the primary defensive mechanism protecting the SGC interior from an unstable gate activation or incoming hostile forces. They are made of thick reinforced steel and titanium composite, capable of withstanding a direct energy weapon discharge. The doors seal automatically when the iris is closed and during red alert conditions. A secondary set of blast doors also exists at the entrance tunnel.

l28-ramp.name: Ramp
l28-ramp.shortDesc: The metal ramp leading up to the Stargate event horizon.
l28-ramp.longDesc: The ramp is the final few metres of Earth that departing SG teams walk before stepping through the Stargate. Made of a non-reactive metal alloy, it leads directly from the Gateroom floor up to the event horizon. Teams departing on missions and travellers arriving from other worlds all pass over this ramp. Over the course of the show it becomes one of the most symbolically significant locations in the series.

l28-laboratory.name: Laboratory
l28-laboratory.shortDesc: Scientific analysis lab for studying alien artefacts and technology.
l28-laboratory.longDesc: The laboratory on Level 28 is primarily used by Dr. Daniel Jackson, Dr. Samantha Carter, and visiting scientists to analyse alien artefacts, biological samples, and recovered technology brought back through the Stargate. It contains advanced spectrographic, chemical, and electronic analysis equipment. Several major discoveries — including decoding Ancient tablet inscriptions and reverse-engineering Goa'uld technology — took place here.

l28-observation-deck.name: Observation Deck
l28-observation-deck.shortDesc: Secondary observation point overlooking the Gateroom from ground level.
l28-observation-deck.longDesc: The Observation Deck provides a ground-level viewing area for the Gateroom, separate from the elevated Control Room window. It is used during VIP visits, diplomatic meetings, or when additional personnel need to monitor gate operations without crowding the Control Room. Government officials, Pentagon liaisons, and alien allies have observed gate activations from this position.

l28-staging-room.name: Staging Room
l28-staging-room.shortDesc: Pre-mission assembly area adjacent to the Gateroom.
l28-staging-room.longDesc: The Staging Room is where SG teams form up immediately before gate departure. Final equipment checks, last-minute briefings, and pre-mission medical clearances happen here. It sits directly between the main corridor and the Gateroom blast doors, making it the last controlled space before personnel step through the wormhole.

l28-toilets.name: Restrooms
l28-toilets.longDesc: Standard SGC restroom facilities on Level 28.

l28-round-hallway.name: Round Hallway
l28-round-hallway.shortDesc: Curved corridor linking major Level 28 sections.
l28-round-hallway.longDesc: The Round Hallway is a distinctive curved corridor that connects the main Level 28 areas — the Control Room, Gateroom approach, and the tunnel corridor leading to the elevators and armory. Its rounded shape is an architectural feature of the original missile silo construction. It is frequently seen in chase sequences and tense confrontations throughout the series.

l28-elevator-surface.name: Elevator to Surface
l28-elevator-surface.shortDesc: Left elevator shaft — direct access to the surface of Cheyenne Mountain.
l28-elevator-surface.longDesc: This elevator connects Level 28 directly to the surface of Cheyenne Mountain. It is the primary route for SG teams arriving from or departing to the outside world, and for supply runs and VIP transfers. During lockdown, this shaft is sealed and guarded at both ends.

l28-elevator-l27.name: Elevator to Level 27
l28-elevator-l27.shortDesc: Right elevator shaft — connects Level 28 to Level 27 and upper sublevels.
l28-elevator-l27.longDesc: This elevator provides transit between the gate operations floor (Level 28) and the administrative level above (Level 27), as well as all other sublevels of the complex. It is the standard route taken by personnel moving between the Briefing Room, General's Office, and the Gateroom.

l28-outer-corridor.name: Outer Corridor
l28-outer-corridor.shortDesc: Perimeter corridor running around the full outside of Level 28.
l28-outer-corridor.longDesc: The outer corridor forms the full perimeter of Level 28, wrapping around the Gateroom, Control Room, and all adjacent sections. It serves as the primary access route for security patrols, emergency egress, and logistics movement between sections without passing through the high-security interior rooms. Multiple blast doors and security checkpoints segment the corridor during lockdown.

l28-armory.name: Armory
l28-armory.shortDesc: Weapons storage and issue point for SG teams departing on missions.
l28-armory.longDesc: The SGC Armory on Level 28 is the primary weapons storage and issue point for all SG teams. Personnel collect personal sidearms, P90 submachine guns, and mission-specific ordnance here before departing through the Stargate. The room maintains inventory of standard Earth weapons as well as select recovered alien weaponry cleared for field use. It sits adjacent to the Round Hallway for rapid access before gate departure.

l28-freight-elevator.name: Freight Elevator
l28-freight-elevator.shortDesc: Heavy-capacity elevator for large equipment and alien artefacts.
l28-freight-elevator.longDesc: The freight elevator provides heavy-load vertical transit capability within the SGC. It is used to move large alien artefacts, heavy weapons, vehicles, and bulk supplies between sublevels without occupying the main personnel elevators. The platform is wide enough to accommodate a Goa'uld death glider fuselage or equivalent-sized cargo.

l28-storage-room.name: Storage Room
l28-storage-room.shortDesc: General-purpose storage adjacent to the elevator shafts.
l28-storage-room.longDesc: The storage room on Level 28 serves as a general-purpose holding area for equipment, supplies, and gear staged near the elevator access points. It provides quick access for loading and unloading cargo from the freight elevator and is used to temporarily stage mission equipment before it is moved to the Armory, Laboratory, or Staging Room.
